title: -Contract No. 108037-1 Amendment: MSR Mechanical, LLC for On-Call Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract amendment with
MSR Mechanical, LLC for on-call HVAC maintenance and repair to increase the
contract amount by $50,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $530,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There are funds available in the adopted FY2026 Public Works Budget (Fund 673) for
HVAC maintenance and repairs services. Contract 108037-1 is set to expire on March
31, 2026, and will not be extended past the expiration date.
sections.current_situation:
MSR Mechanical LLC (MSR) provides a comprehensive range of on-call and
emergency heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) services to support the
Public Works Department. Services include inspection, trouble shooting, installation,
maintenance and research into repairs or replacements. Timely inspection and
maintenance of ventilation systems in City buildings is critical to ensuring optimum
system performance.
Recently MSR performed a major HVAC repair and installation project for the City’s
Public Safety Building. This work was completed recently, but the costs associated with
this work went unexpectedly above budget due to unforeseen repairs and parts needed.
To complete payment on the already incurred costs, the department is asking for a
contract amendment for the additional funds.
This contract will support the City’s facilities and is in alignment with the City’s Strategic
Plan, advancing our goal to provide state-of-the-art, well-maintained infrastructure,
amenities, and facilities.
sections.background:
The City contracted with MSR Mechanical in 2015. MSR has provided quality service
throughout the duration of the contract. In May 2025, the City conducted a solicitation
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract: Contract No. 108037-1 Amendment: MSR Mechanical, CONSENT CALENDAR
LLC for on-call heating, ventilation and air conditioning services January 27, 2026
for HVAC services and awarded contracts to two vendors, Matrix HG and Prime
Mechanical. MSR Mechanical’s contract is set to expire on March 31, 2026, though all
service with MSR Mechanical has ceased upon the completion of HVAC work at the
Public Safety Building in November 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Regular maintenance and repair of HVAC systems is essential for optimizing
performance, prolonging equipment lifespan, and minimizing environmental impacts.
Well-maintained HVAC systems operate more efficiently, which leads to reduced energy
consumption and carbon emissions. Regular service prevents the leaking of refrigerants
into the atmosphere, which are damaging to the ozone layer and contribute to climate
change. Optimized HVAC systems also improve indoor air quality and provide air
circulation benefits.
sections.rationale:
Required HVAC repairs and installation work has been completed at the Public
Safety Building and this amendment allows the City to complete payment with MSR
Mechanical. The City requires reliable and responsive HVAC maintenance and
repair services to maintain the heating, ventilation and air-conditioning needs of City
facilities.
sections.alternatives:
The City must remit payment, due to the completion of services. While the additional
expenses were unexpected, funds are readily available and budgeted for HVAC
services. The contractor is knowledgeable about and familiar with the City's equipment
and has been highly responsive during emergency situations.
CONTACT PERSON
Joy Brown, Operations Manager, (510) 981-6629
Aaron Baker, Facilities Maintenance Superintendent, (510) 981-6452
Tucker Brofft, Senior Management Analyst, (510) 981-5375
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 108037-1 AMENDMENT: MSR MECHANICAL, LLC FOR ON-CALL
HEATING, VENTILATION AND AIR CONDITIONING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City owns and operates buildings with heating, cooling and ventilation
systems which regulate the temperature and quality of indoor air; and
WHEREAS, staff rely upon HVAC services provided by supplemental contractors to
help maintain properly performing HVAC systems for optimal indoor air quality and
reduced energy usage; and
WHEREAS, the City and MSR Mechanical, LLC entered into Contact No. 108037-1 for
on-call and emergency HVAC services, effective November 9, 2015, and extended
through June 30, 2022; and
WHEREAS, on May 31, 2022, Council authorized Resolution No. 70,393-N.S. to
increase the contract by $50,000 for a new not to exceed amount of $130,000 and
extend the contract term to June 30, 2024; and
WHEREAS, on October 10, 2023, Council authorized Resolution No. 71,075-N.S. to
increase the contract by $350,000 for a new not to exceed amount of $480,000 and
extend the contract term to June 30, 2025; and
WHEREAS, on May 20, 2025, City Manager authorized an extension to contract
108037-1 from an expiration date of June 30, 2025 to December 31, 2025; and
WHEREAS, on December 1, 2025, City Manager authorized an extension to contract
108037-1 from an expiration date of December 31, 2025, to March 31, 2026; and
WHEREAS, funding is available in the adopted FY2026 Public Works Budget (Fund
673) for HVAC maintenance and repairs services.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute an amendment with MSR Mechanical, LLC to
provide on-call and emergency HVAC services for the City’s HVAC systems, increasing
the Contract amount by $50,000 for a total contract not to exceed amount of $530,000.
